label: OpenPhone
subtitle: call source
desc: OpenPhone is the VoIP system that originates all calls for Heartwood Custom Builders. Call recordings and metadata travel through Zapier rather than being ingested directly, so OpenPhone itself has no direct API integration in the codebase. The pipeline depends on OpenPhone providing a recording_url field that transcribe-deepgram fetches as raw audio, plus structured call metadata including phone numbers, direction, duration, and transcript text. The canonical interaction_id (formatted as cll_...) originates from this system.

label: Zapier
subtitle: webhook relay
desc: Zapier acts as the webhook bridge between OpenPhone and the Camber pipeline, delivering call payloads to the zapier-call-ingest Edge Function via HTTP POST. Authentication is dual-mode: the canonical path validates X-Edge-Secret against EDGE_SHARED_SECRET, with a transitional legacy fallback checking X-Secret against ZAPIER_INGEST_SECRET. Zapier metadata headers (X-Zapier-Zap-ID, X-Zapier-Run-ID, X-Zapier-Timestamp, Idempotency-Key) are captured and stamped onto every forwarded payload for traceability. The function retries once on downstream 401 after 500ms, then relays the response status verbatim.

label: Deepgram
subtitle: Nova-2 ASR
desc: Deepgram handles speech-to-text transcription via the transcribe-deepgram Edge Function (v5), calling the Nova-2 model (overridable via DEEPGRAM_MODEL env var) with diarization, punctuation, utterances, and smart formatting enabled. The API key is retrieved at runtime from the Supabase api_keys table rather than a static env var. A key feature is dynamic vocabulary injection: up to 100 active terms from the transcription_vocab table are injected per-request as keyword:boost pairs, improving recognition of construction-specific terms. Transcriptions are written to transcripts_comparison for A/B testing, not to the main pipeline tables.

label: OpenAI
subtitle: GPT-4o-mini + embed-3
desc: OpenAI powers two distinct capabilities. The segment-llm function calls GPT-4o-mini (max_tokens=1024) to identify project-switch boundaries in call transcripts, with a retry on unexpectedly single-span results and a deterministic fallback split. The embed-facts and journal-embed-backfill functions use text-embedding-3-small (1536 dimensions) to generate vector embeddings of project facts and journal claims, enabling downstream semantic similarity lookups in context-assembly. All functions authenticate via the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable.

label: Anthropic
subtitle: Haiku + Sonnet 4.5
desc: Anthropic Claude models handle the high-judgment tasks in the pipeline. Claude 3 Haiku (claude-3-haiku-20240307, temp=0) powers six functions: ai-router for project attribution decisions, journal-extract for knowledge claim extraction, generate-summary for call summaries, striking-detect for signal perception, chain-detect for temporal clustering, and loop-closure for resolving open items. Claude Sonnet 4.5 (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) handles journal-consolidate where deeper reasoning is needed to classify claim relationships. All functions authenticate via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

label: Gmail API
subtitle: 30-day lookback
desc: The Gmail API integration in gmail-context-lookup fetches recent email threads for a contact as weak corroboration evidence. It enforces a strict budget of 5 Gmail API calls per invocation (1 list + 4 message GETs) with a 30-day lookback window. Auth resolves through a three-tier fallback: static access token, OAuth refresh token flow, or service account JWT. Results are cached for 1 hour in gmail_context_cache, keyed by SHA-256 of the contact email set. The function is fully fail-open: any auth failure or API error returns empty email_context with a 200 OK.

label: GitHub
subtitle: CI/CD + repos
desc: GitHub hosts the camber repository and provides four CI/CD workflows via Actions. deploy-edge-functions.yml auto-deploys all functions on push to main using Supabase CLI v2.76.8. deno-ci.yml enforces code quality (fmt + lint), runs a live pipeline replay proof-pack, and executes invariant gate checks. secret-scan.yml scans PR diffs for leaked credentials. camber-preflight.yml validates auth patterns and tool presence. Secrets like SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN and EDGE_SHARED_SECRET are injected at runtime.

label: Supabase Auth
subtitle: JWT verification
desc: Supabase Auth provides JWT-based user authentication for human-facing endpoints. The review-resolve function is the only endpoint using verify_jwt=true, verifying tokens via auth.getUser() and checking against an ALLOWED_EMAILS allowlist. All other pipeline functions bypass JWT verification entirely (verify_jwt=false in config.toml) and rely solely on X-Edge-Secret for machine-to-machine auth. The shared _shared/auth.ts module implements constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks on the shared secret.

label: zapier-call-ingest
subtitle: 226 lines
desc: zapier-call-ingest (v1.4.0) is the sole external entry point for call data. It validates callers using X-Edge-Secret (canonical) or X-Secret (legacy transitional), logging legacy usage to diagnostic_logs. After auth, it unwraps the payload from a payload_json envelope if present, stamps Zapier tracking metadata (zap ID, run ID, timestamp, idempotency key) as provenance, and forwards the enriched payload to process-call with both the service role key and X-Edge-Secret. On a 401 from process-call it retries exactly once after 500ms. The response proxies back to Zapier with an _ingest metadata block.

label: process-call
subtitle: v4.3.7 · 1,177 lines
desc: process-call (v4.3.7, 1,177 lines) is the core ingestion engine. It enforces idempotency via the idempotency_keys table, normalizes the raw payload, resolves phone direction (owner vs other-party), and runs a quality gate checking for malformed IDs and empty transcripts. The heart of the function is a 6-source candidate project collection: direct CRM assignments, historical call frequency affinity, existing project fallback, word-boundary transcript scanning, fuzzy similarity via RPC, and mentioned-contact expansion. Candidates are scored and ranked, but process-call never writes project_id to interactions (Stopline 1). A junk-call prefilter flags voicemail and low-content calls. If the gate passes, it fires segment-call as fire-and-forget.

label: transcribe-deepgram
subtitle: v5 · 318 lines
desc: transcribe-deepgram (v5) is a standalone function NOT in the main pipeline chain. It accepts a recording_url, fetches the audio as binary, and submits it to Deepgram with dynamic vocabulary injection from the transcription_vocab table (up to 100 terms, sorted by boost score). Transcription uses Nova-2 with diarization, punctuation, and smart formatting. Output is formatted with SPEAKER_N labels and includes word-level timing and confidence. Results are upserted to transcripts_comparison for A/B testing across engine variants (keywords_on vs keywords_off).

label: phone modules
subtitle: phone_lookup + direction
desc: Two small helper modules handle phone normalization for process-call. phone_lookup.ts exports normalizePhoneForLookup(), which strips non-digit characters and returns the last 10 digits to handle E.164 numbers with country codes. phone_direction.ts exports resolveCallPartyPhones(), which uses explicit owner_phone/other_party_phone fields first, then falls back to from/to fields interpreted by call direction: inbound treats to_phone as the owner (company line) and from_phone as the other party. When direction is unknown, it makes a best-effort assignment and emits a data_quality_missing_direction warning.

label: segment-call
subtitle: v2.5.3 · 934 lines
desc: segment-call (v2.5.3) is the orchestration hub between ingestion and per-span analysis. It enforces the reseed rule (409 if attributions already exist), fetches the transcript from calls_raw if not provided, sanitizes control characters, and calls segment-llm for boundaries. It then upserts N conversation_spans rows and processes each span SEQUENTIALLY (not in parallel): context-assembly (blocking) then ai-router (blocking). After successful attribution, it fires three async hooks: striking-detect on every span, journal-extract when decision=assign, and generate-summary once per call after all spans complete. Any span failure returns 500 chain_failed (fail-closed).

label: segment-llm
subtitle: v1.4.0 · 897 lines
desc: segment-llm (v1.4.0) is a pure computation function with ZERO database writes. It calls GPT-4o-mini with a construction-specific prompt to identify project-switch boundaries in the transcript. After LLM response, it applies a 10-step guardrail chain: clamp boundaries, sort, make contiguous, enforce first=0/last=len, merge undersized segments, enforce max_segments, remove zero-length, and split oversized segments (>3000 chars) at natural punctuation near project anchor terms. If a long transcript yields only one span, it retries with a stricter prompt, then falls back to deterministic equal-splitting. On LLM failure, it returns a single full-call fallback span.

label: _shared/
subtitle: 4 modules · ~380 lines
desc: Four reusable modules imported across the pipeline. auth.ts implements requireEdgeSecret() with constant-time comparison and X-Source allowlist validation. id_guard.ts validates interaction_id (cll_...) and span_id (UUID) formats with OCR-confusable detection. llm_json.ts provides a 5-attempt JSON parser: strict parse, strip code fences, sanitize control chars, remove trailing commas, and extract named arrays from partial output. junk_call_prefilter.ts classifies calls as junk based on voicemail phrases, connection failure phrases, and low-content heuristics (word count <20, single speaker, absence of construction terms).

label: context-assembly
subtitle: v2.1.1 · 3,279 lines
desc: Context Assembly (v2.1.1, 3,279 lines) is the largest function in the pipeline. Called per-span by segment-call, it assembles a richly structured context_package from 12 evidence sources: direct CRM assignments, call-frequency affinity, existing attributions, alias matching, trigram fuzzy search, full-text search, journal claims cross-reference, place mentions, geo proximity, email context, world model facts, and homeowner override. Candidates are scored using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with a strict priority ladder that ranks source_strength above affinity weight (a fix for the Permar-class misattribution bug). The homeowner override gate deterministically assigns if a unique homeowner is found without contradictory anchors in the transcript.

label: gmail-context-lookup
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 816 lines
desc: gmail-context-lookup (v1.0.0) is called synchronously by context-assembly with an 8-second timeout and fail-open design. It queries the Gmail API for recent email threads involving the contact, extracting subject keywords (stopword-filtered), project alias mentions (word-boundary matched), and dollar amounts via regex. Results are cached for 1 hour in gmail_context_cache keyed by SHA-256 of the contact email set. The function supports three auth modes in priority order: service account JWT, OAuth refresh token, and static access token. Any failure returns empty email_context rather than an error.

label: Context Retrievers
subtitle: 6 modules · 2,025 lines
desc: Six retrieval modules power context-assembly. claim_crossref.ts (1,306 lines) cross-references journal claims using IDF-weighted semantic overlap with tagged term categories (dollar amounts at 3x weight, proper nouns at 2x, construction terms at 1.5x). rrf_fusion.ts implements Reciprocal Rank Fusion across four channels (structured, FTS, trigram, vector) using the Cormack formula score = 1/(k + rank). trigram_retrieval.ts uses pg_trgm similarity for fuzzy project name matching. fts_retrieval.ts runs full-text search against project_facts. homeowner_override.ts provides the deterministic homeowner assignment when a unique link exists without contradictory anchors.

label: ai-router
subtitle: v1.15.0 · 1,822 lines
desc: ai-router (v1.15.0, 1,822 lines) is the attribution engine. It takes a context_package from context-assembly and uses Claude 3 Haiku (temp=0) to score candidates and assign spans to projects. The 3-band confidence policy: >=0.75 auto-assigns, 0.40-0.74 can safe-low-assign with strong anchors (homeowner override or smoking-gun tier), 0.25-0.40 goes to review, <0.25 produces no attribution. Before LLM inference it applies a junk-call prefilter; after inference, 8 deterministic guardrails run in sequence. The function respects the monotonic lock (human > AI) and fires journal-extract as a belt-and-suspenders hook for replay paths.

label: Guardrail Battery
subtitle: 8 modules · 1,417 lines
desc: Eight deterministic modules applied in sequence after LLM inference. id_guardrails.ts normalizes IDs with NFKC Unicode and Cyrillic confusable mapping. alias_guardrails.ts downgrades assign to review when only generic material aliases support the decision. bizdev_guardrails.ts gates prospect/bizdev calls requiring commitment language for assign. bethany_winship_guardrail.ts handles an address-specific force-assignment. homeowner_override_gate.ts provides deterministic homeowner bypass. rrf_reranker.ts reorders candidates using a 5-tier evidence weight system. rrf_tier_guardrail.ts downgrades weak-tier assignments to review and floors smoking-gun hits at 0.85. world_model_facts.ts adds optional fact corroboration when WORLD_MODEL_FACTS_ENABLED=true.

label: review-resolve
subtitle: v3.1.0 · 203 lines
desc: review-resolve (v3.1.0) is the only user-facing endpoint (verify_jwt=true). It accepts JWT-authenticated requests from the review UI, verifying tokens via auth.getUser() — never by decoding JWT payloads directly. The core is a single call to the resolve_review_item RPC, which atomically in one transaction: updates span_attributions (applied_project_id, attribution_lock=human), closes the review_queue row, inserts an override_log audit entry, and propagates the project to scheduler_items and journal_claims. Human locks are monotonic and can never be downgraded; duplicate resolves are treated as no-ops.

label: review-triage
subtitle: review queue UI
desc: Review queue management endpoint providing list, stats, and dismiss operations for pending review items. Used by morning-digest for queue pressure metrics and by the human review UI to browse pending items. Reads from review_queue, span_attributions, and conversation_spans to build contextual review cards. Auth: X-Edge-Secret.

label: generate-summary
subtitle: v1.0.2 · 682 lines
desc: generate-summary (v1.0.2) produces the human-readable call summary and structured follow-up items after the full span pipeline completes. It assembles a prompt from the transcript (capped at 12,000 chars), span-level attributions, and contact/project metadata, then asks Claude 3 Haiku (configurable via GENERATE_SUMMARY_MODEL) for a 2-4 sentence summary and up to 12 prioritized follow-ups (each with title, action, owner, due hint, priority, and evidence quote). JSON parsing uses a three-attempt strategy: strict parse, repair pass (fix unquoted strings, trailing commas), and regex-based fallback extracting fields independently.

label: striking-detect
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 383 lines
desc: striking-detect (v1.0.0) is a separate perception channel with NO effect on attribution. Fire-and-forget per span, it uses Claude 3 Haiku to score 8 signal types: decision_point, scope_change, financial_signal, relationship_tension, commitment, escalation, surprise, and threshold. Scoring bands: 0-0.2 routine, 0.3-0.5 mild, 0.6-0.7 notable, 0.8-1.0 highly striking. Every signal quote is validated against the actual transcript — fabricated quotes are silently dropped. Spans under 50 characters are skipped entirely. Results feed the morning-digest dashboard.

label: journal-extract
subtitle: v1.3.0 · 994 lines
desc: journal-extract (v1.3.0) captures structured knowledge from attributed spans. Claude 3 Haiku extracts claims across 10 types (commitment, deadline, decision, blocker, requirement, preference, concern, fact, question, update) with 7 epistemic statuses and warrant levels. Transcripts are capped at 8,000 chars to prevent Haiku timeouts. JSON parsing uses a 5-attempt strategy with LLM retry on parse failure. Pre-flight checks: closed-project filter, idempotency (existing claims for span), and an optional SKIP_NON_ASSIGN_WRITES gate. Claims are inserted via the insert_journal_claims_dedup RPC. On success, fires journal-consolidate.

label: journal-consolidate
subtitle: v1.0.2 · 519 lines
desc: journal-consolidate (v1.0.2) maintains the project knowledge graph using Claude Sonnet 4.5 (the most capable model in the pipeline, reflecting the semantic complexity of the task). It compares freshly extracted claims against up to 100 existing confirmed claims for the same project and classifies each as: new, supersedes (temporal priority), corroborates (increases warrant level), or conflicts (requires human review, subtyped as factual/schedule/decision/cost). A notable output is cross_project_signals: when a claim implies resource movement across projects, it flags the implied project for future multi-project coordination.

label: chain-detect
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 493 lines
desc: chain-detect (v1.0.0) identifies temporal call chains — clusters of calls from the same contact on the same day. It takes an AI-forward approach where Claude 3 Haiku judges significance rather than using threshold heuristics. The model selects from 7 chain pattern types: crisis_burst, follow_up_sequence, routine_daily, coordination_cluster, escalation_chain, decision_sequence, or single_call. The design recognizes that 3 calls in 20 minutes is fundamentally different from 3 calls over 8 hours. Results are persisted to the call_chains table.

label: loop-closure
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 416 lines
desc: loop-closure (v1.0.0) resolves open action items by matching new call transcripts against a project"s inventory of open loops. Claude 3 Haiku evaluates up to 50 open loops per project, requiring a confidence threshold of 0.75 and a specific transcript excerpt as evidence before closing any loop. The design enforces evidence-first policy: someone saying "I will look into it" does NOT close a loop. Accepted closures are written to journal_open_loops with the closure_evidence column populated, creating an append-only audit trail.

label: admin-reseed
subtitle: v1.8.0 · 1,718 lines
desc: admin-reseed (v1.8.0, 1,718 lines) is the second-largest function, responsible for re-chunking an interaction"s spans when segmentation was wrong. Three modes: resegment_only, resegment_and_reroute (re-run full attribution), and reseed_and_close_loop (integrity guarantees — retries missing attributions, inserts fallback review rows). A key safety feature is human lock carryforward: if Chad manually corrected a span, that lock is preserved by mapping it to the positionally equivalent new span. Supports force=true for cascade deletion of all dependent data. Fails closed on any write failure.

label: shadow-replay
subtitle: v0.1.0 · 231 lines
desc: shadow-replay (v0.1.0) is a lightweight A/B testing harness. Given a real interaction_id, it fetches the original transcript and metadata, wraps it in a new payload with a synthetic cll_SHADOW_<timestamp>_<rand> ID, and POSTs it to process-call as if it were a fresh call. The shadow record carries is_shadow=true and _shadow_replay_meta provenance so it can be isolated in the database. A dry_run mode previews the payload without triggering the pipeline. This is how engineers test pipeline changes against real transcripts without affecting production.

label: gt-apply
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 359 lines
desc: gt-apply (v1.0.0) is the batch ground-truth correction endpoint used by Claude agents on Chad"s behalf. It accepts up to 100 corrections, resolving span references by span_id or interaction_id+span_index. Each correction calls the apply_gt_correction RPC, which writes attribution_lock=human to span_attributions and appends an override_log audit row. The database-level monotonic lock trigger prevents any correction from ever being downgraded. Returns per-item results with HTTP 207 on partial failures.

label: morning-digest
subtitle: v1.2.0 · 376 lines
desc: morning-digest (v1.2.0) is a read-only daily status report that never writes to the database. It assembles five sections: top striking signals by project, open journal loops by project, review queue pressure (pending count + oldest items), recent journal claims (last 24h by type and project), and pipeline health metrics (calls ingested, spans created, summary generation rate, journal run status). Joins across 11 tables to give the operator a one-glance view of both business intelligence and pipeline health.

label: alias-scout
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 882 lines
desc: alias-scout (v1.0.0) is the automated alias discovery job. Three strategies: (A) finds active projects with zero aliases and generates name-stem + street-name candidates from metadata, (B) enriches projects with only auto-generated aliases by combining client name and street, and (C) mines the last 30 days of attributed transcripts for capitalized terms (4+ chars) appearing in 3+ distinct transcripts for the same project. New candidates are deduplicated against existing aliases and pending suggestions before insertion. Supports dry_run mode.

label: alias-review
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 560 lines
desc: alias-review (v1.0.0) is the approval interface for alias candidates surfaced by alias-scout. GET returns pending suggestions enriched with collision detection — for each candidate, it queries v_project_alias_lookup to find conflicts with other projects. POST processes approve/reject in batches up to 100: approval promotes via the promote_alias RPC (falling back to direct INSERT), rejection updates status. Designed for Claude agents acting on Chad"s behalf.

label: alias-hygiene
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 442 lines
desc: alias-hygiene (v1.0.0) runs three cleanup operations: (1) retires aliases for closed/inactive projects by setting active=false via RPC, (2) expires stale pending suggestions older than 30 days, and (3) scans v_project_alias_lookup for cross-project alias collisions where the same string maps to multiple active projects. Returns a collision report for human attention. Supports dry_run mode. Designed to run as a periodic cron task to keep alias data trustworthy.

label: embed-facts
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 264 lines
desc: embed-facts (v1.0.0) generates vector embeddings for project_facts using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536 dimensions). Backfill mode processes up to 500 unembedded rows per invocation; single mode embeds a specific fact by ID. Facts are composed as fact_kind + JSON payload text, batched in groups of 10 with 100ms inter-batch delays for rate limiting, and validated for exactly 1536 dimensions before writing. Powers the semantic retrieval path in context-assembly via the pgvector cosine distance operator.

label: journal-embed-backfill
subtitle: v1.0.0 · 406 lines
desc: journal-embed-backfill (v1.0.0) backfills vector embeddings on journal_claims for semantic search. For each eligible claim, it builds a structured search_text by concatenating claim_type, epistemic_status, testimony_type, speaker_label, and claim_text (capped at 1400 chars). Calls OpenAI text-embedding-3-small in configurable batches (default 20, max 50). Accepts optional project_id and call_id filters for targeted backfills, and a force flag to re-embed existing embeddings when switching model versions.

label: Core Tables
subtitle: interactions · calls_raw · contacts · projects
desc: The four core tables form the foundational data layer. interactions is the canonical call record holding identity fields, timing, AI-generated summaries, and scheduler metadata — it is the linchpin that conversation_spans, journal_claims, and review_queue all reference. calls_raw is the raw archive of every webhook payload with full transcript text, raw JSON snapshot, and recording URL. contacts maps phone numbers to people (clients, subcontractors, vendors, staff). projects is the master list of construction projects with name, status, address, and aliases — the attribution target the entire pipeline routes calls toward.

label: Span Tables
subtitle: conversation_spans · span_attributions
desc: The two span tables are the single source of truth (SSOT) for all AI routing output. conversation_spans holds one row per logical segment with character offsets, transcript text, segmenter version, and a generation counter for reseed history; a unique constraint on (interaction_id, span_index) WHERE NOT is_superseded enforces clean generational history. span_attributions holds the routing decision: which project, at what confidence, the decision type, attribution_lock (human/AI/null), and full provenance. The monotonic lock trigger prevents any write from downgrading human to AI, making human corrections permanently authoritative.

label: Review / Override
subtitle: review_queue · override_log · event_audit
desc: Three tables form the human oversight and audit layer. review_queue holds spans where the AI declined to guess — with reason codes, context payload, and lifecycle status (pending/resolved/dismissed). override_log is the append-only audit trail for every human correction, capturing before/after values, requester, idempotency key, and an effects_receipt JSON blob. event_audit records pipeline stage tracing (function, status, duration, errors) functioning as a pre-pipeline firewall log.

label: Pipeline Auxiliary
subtitle: idempotency · event_audit · striking · chains
desc: Four tables support pipeline reliability and evaluation. idempotency_keys prevents duplicate processing via atomic upsert returning is_duplicate. striking_signals stores per-span perception scores (0-1) with typed signal arrays and a unique index on span_id+model_id+prompt_version. transcripts_comparison holds multi-engine transcript variants for systematic quality comparison across Deepgram, Whisper, and other engines. call_chains links related interactions from the same contact for multi-call reasoning.

label: Journal Tables
subtitle: claims · runs · loops · conflicts
desc: The journal tables store extracted call intelligence. journal_claims holds one row per factual assertion with claim type (10 types), epistemic metadata, speaker attribution, and a dedup unique index on (call_id, claim_type, claim_text, project_id). journal_runs audits each extraction invocation. journal_open_loops tracks unresolved items (questions, blockers, follow-ups) grouped by project and surfaced in morning-digest. journal_claim_conflicts records contradicting claim pairs with a resolved flag for triage.

label: Context Tables
subtitle: fanout · affinity · geo · email cache
desc: Pre-computed signals feeding context-assembly. contact_fanout classifies contacts by project count (anchored=1, semi_anchored=2, drifter=3-4, floater=5+). correspondent_project_affinity stores learned weights per contact-project pairing from interaction history. project_contacts holds explicit CRM assignments (subcontractor, homeowner, vendor links). The geo tables (project_geo, geo_places) provide proximity signals but geo alone can never justify auto-assign. gmail_context_cache stores bounded email thread summaries with a 1-hour TTL.

label: Alias System
subtitle: aliases · suggestions · v_alias_lookup
desc: Three objects form the alias system. project_aliases links projects to spoken-word aliases (street_name, client_name, name_stem, transcript_mined types) with an active boolean for soft-deletes; a trigger auto-generates street-name aliases when a project address is set. suggested_aliases is the review queue for candidates from alias-scout. v_project_alias_lookup is the unified view used at query time — it unions the aliases table with legacy projects.aliases[], filters to active client projects, and excludes blocklisted terms.

label: World Model
subtitle: project_facts + embeddings
desc: The world model stores durable, timestamped facts about projects with two temporal fields: as_of_at (when the fact was true) and observed_at (when it was recorded), enabling AS_OF retrieval so context-assembly can pull facts known at the time of a call. Each fact has a flexible fact_payload JSONB blob typed by fact_kind, plus provenance pointers to the source span and interaction. embed-facts adds a 1536-dimension vector embedding, enabling cosine-similarity retrieval in context-assembly via the pgvector operator.

label: Supabase Storage
subtitle: audio files + assets
desc: Audio file storage relies on external URLs rather than native Supabase Storage buckets. Call recordings arrive from OpenPhone (via Zapier) as recording_url fields in the webhook payload, stored directly in calls_raw. The transcribe-deepgram function fetches audio at runtime by making an HTTP GET to that URL and streaming it to the Deepgram API — the audio never lands in a Supabase bucket. This means the system depends on the telephony provider URL stability.

label: SMS Messages
subtitle: public.sms_messages
desc: Canonical inbound/outbound SMS ledger written by zapier-sms-ingest. This table is the durable source for text-message provenance used by reporting and timeline views.

label: OpenPhone Threads
subtitle: public.openphone_threads
desc: Thread-level SMS state keyed to OpenPhone conversation threads. Used to group related messages and preserve phone-channel context.

label: Zapier Lineage View
subtitle: v_calls_raw_zapier_lineage
desc: Read model joining calls_raw payload lineage with Zapier metadata for operator traceability and ingest debugging.

label: Cost Code Taxonomy
subtitle: public.cost_code_taxonomy
desc: Authoritative cost-code catalog for finance workflows. Cost-code assignments and validations must reconcile to this taxonomy.

label: Vendor Cost Summary
subtitle: vendor_cost_code_summary
desc: Materialized rollup of vendor-to-cost-code usage patterns used in finance/cost-code analysis and reporting.

label: Check Postable Cost Code
subtitle: check_postable_cost_code()
desc: Validation function that blocks non-postable cost-code writes. This is the finance guardrail used in intake preflight.

label: resolve_review_item
subtitle: RPC · single-txn
desc: resolve_review_item is the atomic single-transaction RPC for human review decisions. It row-locks the review_queue item, checks for human-lock conflicts, writes an idempotency-keyed audit row to override_log, updates span_attributions with applied_project_id and attribution_lock=human, marks the review resolved, and cascades project_id to null-project scheduler_items and journal_claims. Already-resolved items return was_already_resolved=true rather than failing. Runs as SECURITY DEFINER guaranteeing all-or-nothing semantics.

label: apply_gt_correction
subtitle: RPC · monotonic
desc: apply_gt_correction applies a single ground-truth correction to span_attributions. Called by gt-apply for each correction in a batch, it writes applied_project_id and attribution_lock=human, and appends an override_log audit entry. The database-level enforce_lock_monotonic trigger fires on any lock change, rejecting attempts to move from human (level 3) to AI (level 2) or null (level 0) — making human corrections permanently authoritative regardless of subsequent pipeline reruns.

label: insert_journal_claims_dedup
subtitle: RPC · dedup
desc: insert_journal_claims_dedup is the idempotent batch writer for journal claims. It uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING keyed on a partial unique index (call_id, claim_type, claim_text, project_id) WHERE active=true, preventing duplicate claims from accumulating on retries or reseeds. Also includes resolve_speaker_contact which maps speaker labels from transcripts to contact records. Returns the count of newly inserted rows so the caller knows whether new information was added.

label: Contact RPCs
subtitle: lookup · scan · expand
desc: Three RPCs provide transcript-time lookups for process-call. lookup_contact_by_phone normalizes digits and tries three match strategies: exact, secondary phone, and last-10-digit suffix match for international formats. scan_transcript_for_projects tokenizes the transcript into 4+ character words and calls find_fuzzy_alias_matches (pg_trgm similarity, threshold 0.35) for each. expand_candidates_from_mentions finds contact first names mentioned in the transcript and uses their affinity weights to boost associated projects.

label: Monotonic Lock Trigger
subtitle: enforce_lock_monotonic
desc: enforce_lock_monotonic is the PostgreSQL trigger enforcing Stopline 2: truth promotion is monotonic — human overrides AI; AI must never override human. It fires BEFORE UPDATE on span_attributions when attribution_lock changes, comparing old and new values against a priority map (human=3, AI=2, null=0). If the new level is lower than the old, it raises a Postgres EXCEPTION aborting the transaction. This means no edge function, RPC, or direct SQL can silently downgrade a human correction.

label: GitHub Actions
subtitle: .github/workflows/
desc: Four GitHub Actions workflows. deploy-edge-functions.yml auto-deploys all functions on push to main using Supabase CLI v2.76.8 (pinned to avoid deno.lock incompatibilities) with parallel matrix jobs and endpoint verification. deno-ci.yml enforces fmt + lint on PRs, runs a live pipeline replay proof-pack, and executes invariant gate checks. secret-scan.yml scans diffs for credentials. camber-preflight.yml validates auth patterns (no hardcoded URLs, proper dual-header internal calls).

label: Tests
subtitle: 15+ *_test.ts + fixtures
desc: Testing is primarily shell-script-based rather than unit-test-based, reflecting the integration-heavy nature of edge function pipelines. edge-smoke-test.sh is the post-deploy suite (auth gate + auth pass for all 13 functions). invariant_gates.sh checks four DB invariants. proof_pack.sh and replay_call.sh replay known-good interactions. segmentation_regression_check.sh tests specific problematic transcripts. gt_rerun_proof_harness.sh verifies ground-truth corrections survive reseed cycles.

label: edge-smoke-test.sh
subtitle: auth gate + pass test
desc: edge-smoke-test.sh runs two checks per function across all 13 pipeline endpoints: an auth gate test (no credentials, expect 401/403) and an auth pass test (with X-Edge-Secret, expect 200/400/403/404 — any proving auth worked since the smoke payload uses invalid IDs). Results are color-coded pass/fail/warn, and the script exits 1 on any hard failure. It also serves as the live registry of all pipeline function names and their expected auth behaviors. Can target a single function or test all at once.

label: Claim Guard
subtitle: claim_guard.sh + write scripts
desc: claim_guard.sh is the shared preflight library for all write-mode scripts. require_claim_context enforces that ORIGIN_SESSION (e.g., dev-3) and CLAIM_RECEIPT (must start with claim__) are set before any DB writes — missing either prints example export commands and exits with code 2. write_claim_artifact saves a timestamped claim_context.txt capturing session owner, receipt, ETA, lease, and claimed resource. This implements Charter section 11 collision-prevention at the script level.

label: Pipeline Scripts
subtitle: 50+ scripts/ (Python+Bash)
desc: Over 50 operational and evaluation scripts spanning the pipeline lifecycle. Key categories: replay/backfill (replay_call.sh, shadow_batch_replay.sh, admin_reseed_batch_backfill.py), ground-truth evaluation (gt_batch_runner.sh, gt_pick_fresh_review_items_v1.sh, gt_rerun_proof_harness.sh), invariant/proof scripts (invariant_gates.sh, proof_pack.sh), migration management (migration_apply_guarded.sh, migration_drift_snapshot.sh), and regression harnesses (segmentation_regression_check.sh, temporal_backtest_harness.sh). All write-mode scripts require claim_guard.sh preflight.

label: Migrations
subtitle: 498 SQL (Dec 2025 – Feb 2026)
desc: 498 SQL migration files (Dec 2025 - Feb 2026) managing the full schema evolution. December established core tables (interactions, contacts, projects, calls_raw). January built out journal tables, review_queue, override_log, fuzzy alias functions, and the affinity system. Late January/early February added conversation_spans, span_attributions, the enforce_lock_monotonic trigger, and close-loop machinery. February 2026 added the alias system, world model with vector embeddings, contact_fanout, and ground-truth RPCs.

label: zapier-sms-ingest
subtitle: Edge Function v3
desc: Receives SMS payloads from Zapier and writes sms_messages plus diagnostic logs. Canonical SMS ingestion lane for Flow view evidence.

label: sms-openphone-sync
subtitle: openphone_threads bridge
desc: SMS parity bridge that represents openphone_threads lineage and normalized handoff into zapier-sms-ingest.

label: cost-code-taxonomy
subtitle: public.cost_code_taxonomy
desc: Canonical finance taxonomy table used to validate postable cost codes before write paths continue.

label: vendor-cost-code-summary
subtitle: public.vendor_cost_code_summary
desc: Materialized vendor rollup for cost code posting checks and finance preflight guardrails.

label: promote_journal_claims_to_belief
subtitle: promotion + pointer writes
desc: Runs promote_journal_claims_to_belief and writes belief_claims, promotion_log, journal_review_queue, and claim_pointers.

label: fn_timeline_append
subtitle: project_timeline_events
desc: Appends timeline events into project_timeline_events for activity timeline surfaces.
